biographical_arc.event: At age 14, saw vision of Shiva in Himalayas (Devaldhar, near Bageshwar)
Three months later, vision of Shiva repeated in his room
Born in Bhopal on July 10, 1957, as the sixth child of Shri Himmat Bahadur Sharma and Shrimati Gyani Devi Sharma.
At eight months, spoke his first word 'kutta' (dog), showing early affinity for animals.
Started reading Hindi before age five, preferring adult company over peers.
Family connections included Mahatma Gandhi, Jawaharlal Nehru, and other prominent figures; father was a judge.
Mother Gyani Devi was exceptionally strong-willed and physically strong, with an exalted Mars in her horoscope.
Learned from stories about Hanuman puja, aghoris, tantrics, and the lives of saints like Trailanga Swami and Vivekananda
Witnessed his uncle Shraddhanand Brahmachari's failed tantric curse and subsequent death, learning about the dangers of misuse of tantra
At age 15, became friends with Lalmani Sharma and visited his father to hear about Hanuman's appearance
Grandfather Gulab Singh dies at 97; Sharma witnesses luminous spheres descending and ascending at deathbed
Lives in Sagar, Madhya Pradesh; attends government school, makes friends across castes
Observes friend Hari Om tenderly massaging his mother's blistered hands; feels envy and later tries to replicate scene with his own mother
Slapped by a teacher for inattention; laughs at the absurdity of the situation
Learns about social hierarchy when a constable forbids his son from playing with Sharma due to class differences
Death of elder brother Mahendra; family holds wedding on same day as funeral rites
Reflects on death and meaning of life; existential crisis
Rejects army career citing long hair; family shocked
Reads Autobiography of a Yogi (Dec 1978); decides to dedicate life to Kriya Yoga
Completes M.A. Political Science; remains focused on yoga
Encounter with mysterious ascetic on Mount Amara (summer 1979)
Discovers Kriya Yoga group in Gwalior; meets Sidhoji Rao S.
Fifth Guru of Kriya Yoga lineage, carries authentic teaching into new century.
Emphasizes connection with Mother Earth as main deity.
States that spiritual union is achieved when one's own spirit becomes the Guru and mind becomes student of that Spirit.
Depicted as supremely realized yogi, not a miracle worker or modern saint.
Sharma witnesses Hanuman's appearance during a ritual performed by a 'Master' (a cart-driver) in Sagor.
Sharma's eyesight deteriorates; he retires from government service.
After retirement, Sharma visits Sagor and learns that the Master and devotees have died, but the miracles (healing, children) manifested.
Sharma has a transformative realization about faith: seeing the divine requires a leap of faith; faith is a lightning strike that cannot be ignored.
At age ~16, Sharma pursues a military career, joining the National Cadet Corps, excelling in shooting and marching.
In 1976, Sharma participates in the Republic Day parade in Delhi, hosted at the President's residence.
Sharma meets Harpal Singh Siyal, who recommends 'Autobiography of a Yogi'.
In 1977, Sharma passes the SSB interview (including unusual questions) and is recommended for a commission as Second Lieutenant.
Sharma returns home to learn of his elder brother Mahendra's sudden death from a heart attack at age 32, a blow that initiates his spiritual search.
Born into a large extended family living in a centuries-old, four-story house with 150+ relatives.
Observed a devoted servant couple; the wife's unwavering respect and rituals towards her husband profoundly impressed him.
Lived in Gwalior and later Shivpuri, where a famous astrologer predicted he would fail at everything; he resolved to do nothing.
Had no life line or head line on his palms at birth; they appeared later after he started yoga.
Developed a passion for reading, devouring hundreds of books including the Bible, Indian myths, Jack London, Shakespeare, Voltaire, and Richard Bach's 'Jonathan Livingston Seagull'.
Became convinced that imagination is the most powerful energy; later equated 'willpower' with controlled imagination.
Often skipped school with his cousin to sit on hills overlooking Gwalior, contemplating life.
Barely passed college exams; parents despaired of his future.
He later wrote a book about Time; the passage implies this was his first book.
Gulab Singh (grandfather of Shailendra Sharma) was a jagirdar who killed British soldiers and lost his principality.
Gulab Singh served as a telegraphist in Assam, survived a black fever epidemic.
Gulab Singh fled to Burma, worked for a timber merchant, and read the Bhagavata Purana to the merchant's mother.
While reading the Bhagavata Purana, Gulab Singh experienced spiritual awakening (vairagya).
Gulab Singh went to Allahabad for Kumbh Mela seeking a guru.
Gulab Singh met Rajendra Singh, guru of the Maharaja of Patiala, and received initiation.
Gulab Singh became head of an ashram in Patiala but felt dissatisfied.
Gulab Singh was forcibly married by his family after being found.
Gulab Singh returned to his guru who told him to fulfill worldly duties.
Gulab Singh served as a colonel in the Afghan War (1892), received commendation from Queen Victoria.
Gulab Singh participated in a nationalist conspiracy, forged bills for 200,000 rupees, and was arrested but acquitted.
In Calcutta jail, Gulab Singh met Sri Aurobindo.
Gulab Singh lived a long adventurous life, died old.
Shailendra Sharma (grandson) grew up; as a schoolboy, bandits like Mohar Singh surrendered and were jailed in Gwalior.
First meditation on July 11, 1979 during a fire ceremony (havan). Experienced void and clear realization that self-inquiry is life's purpose.
Met Anandamayi Ma in Vrindavan in late July 1979. Received prasad and teaching 'Karoge to hoga'.
Practiced 2000 rounds of bhastrika daily. In December 1979, experienced out-of-body travel (astral projection).
Traveled to Puri in March 1981 to join Karar Ashram under Swami Hariharananda and learn Kriya Yoga.
Arrives in Varanasi for first meeting with Guru Satya Charan Lahiri (October 1982)
Initiation by Satya Charan Lahiri: activation of energy nodes (chakras) along spine
Guru questions why he does not wear the sacred thread (janeu); admits he never underwent the rite
Guru teaches first level of Kriya; he realizes he already knows the techniques
Guru sends him back to Gwalior to master Khechari Mudra before next level
Family moves to a farm in Gwalior after selling house (November 1982)
Year-long siege by bandits; involvement in armed conflicts
Discovers his pulse stops during practice; gains control over heart rate and blood vessels (January 1983)
Masters Khechari Mudra (March 1983)
During night practice, perceives Kutastha (inner light sphere); recounts to Guru who identifies it as Atma and Paramatma
Encounter with a mysterious sadhu who taught him alchemy and the Maran mantra
Performed Maran mantra ritual at a cremation ground for 21 nights
Experienced a blue flame and a corpse turning its head; completed the ritual
Struggled with inner anger for a year after acquiring the mantra
Confessed to his Guru and was advised to relinquish the mantra; performed ritual on copper plate and threw it into the Ganges
Attended a festival at Bhupendranath Sanyal's ashram in Puri
Met Jwalaprasad Tiwari, who gave him Satya Charan Lahiri's address in Varanasi
Left Puri ashram and returned to Gwalior
Corresponded with Satya Charan Lahiri who advised him to settle worldly matters first
Arrived in Varanasi on 30 Sept 1982, Lahiri Mahasaya's birthday
Met Satya Charan Lahiri and received instruction to come next morning with supplies
Bathed in the Ganges and prepared for initiation
Learns to see human karmas under his Guru's guidance
Observes karma through stories of a former murderer and servant Debi Lal
Studies Samudrika Shastra and other methods to read past karmas from physical traits
Resolves to repay karmic debts and avoid new ones
Reflects on Aghori path while in Varanasi
Sent by Satya Charan Lahiri to visit an Aghori in final meditation (November 1985)
Receives prophecy from Aghori about USSR dissolution and US disunion
Later sees prophecy fulfilled as USSR collapses and spiritual seekers come to India
Disillusionment with the initial ashram (Karrar) - realized practitioners lacked essence and authenticity
Encountered a Sikh travel agent who played a meaningful Bollywood song, lifting his mood
Visited Guru Dham ashram in Puri, met Shailendranath Mukherjee, a disciple of Bhupendranath Sanyal
Read Lahiri Mahasaya's commentary on the Gita, realizing the techniques he learned were not authentic Kriya
Left the ashram to test divine providence: wandered in the jungle without food
Collapsed under a banyan tree, slept for days; local women brought food and a young girl fed him with her hands
Had a realization about women's compassion as a manifestation of the Life Force
Returned to the ashram; tension with the manager
Went to the beach at night and encountered two winged humanoid creatures emerging from the sea, then diving back
Family moves back to city in 1985
First samadhi during night sadhana: consciousness expands, heart and breath stop, experiences collective human emotions and past lives
Rapid aging and depression following samadhi; physical and mental exhaustion
Recovery through physical exercises (weightlifting, mahamudra) to reverse aging
Learns to block waves of human thoughts and emotions after samadhi
Realizes samadhi as a meeting with death; body began aging due to cessation of heartbeat and breath
Reveals the illusory nature of religious and social institutions
After samadhi, can see his own soul (Jiva) as a golden figure when eyes closed
18 months of intense practice, gains full control of pulse and heart rate.
Masters Khechari mudra and writes to guru; receives invitation to Varanasi to receive second kriya.
In April 1984, learns second level kriya (amantra and samantra) from Satyacharan Lahiri.
During service to Lahiri Mahasaya's statue, the marble turns to warm flesh; he is startled.
Satyacharan Lahiri declares him the successor (fifth guru of the parampara) despite being only 26 and a novice of 1.5 years.
Initially confused by the appointment, but accepts; finds that he can give profound answers during darśans.
After April 1984, his Guru initiated only a few disciples; later Sharma gave diksha to hundreds in his Guru's presence.
He returned to Benares 2-3 times a year for several weeks over three years.
He gave Kriya initiation to about 400 disciples in his Guru's presence.
Received subtle instruction from Guru on answering questions and reading karma.
Silently touched Lahiri Mahasaya's statue but felt only cool marble.
After returning from Puri, warned acquaintances about ineffective techniques at Karar ashram.
Met SR (Siddhoji Rao), who later became his 25th disciple.
Initiated first disciple Bhardwaj Dubey in Gwalior.
Old friend Vvas-ji (Shivanath Vyas) recognized him as guru due to a vision; initiated Vvas-ji and later Upendra and Shastri-ji.
Fame spread; disciples began visiting his room, surprising family.
Attended Superman movie in Gwalior; two elderly pandits prostrated at his feet.
Mother saw a golden flame around his head (first sight of his aura).
Learned to control his aura; aura later turned greenish, then became white Shivling-like.
Witnessed Guru's stern dealing with a crippled former criminal disguised as a priest.
Encountered an Aghori, which seeded his future connection with Aghora and Parashurama.
Received personal message from Parashurama via another's dream, 19 years after the Aghori meeting.
Obtained a mystical Sri Yantra with precise installation instructions.
In March 1986, visited Kashi Vishwanath temple during Mahashivaratri, where he fell onto the Shivalingam, interpreted as a darshan and offering of himself to Shiva.
Under Satyacharan Lahiri's guidance from November 1985 to March 1986, made several fateful visits to people and places.
Practiced Kriya intensely, extending meditation to 20 hours daily from April 1985, overcoming pain, and experiencing inner time.
After six months of intense sadhana, pain dissolved, and he achieved a state where the body became mind.
Lived at maternal uncle's house after brother Gyandendra left
Practiced weightlifting with cousin, spent all money
Gave last 5 rupees to a crying balloon seller boy
Received 300 rupees from grateful father as first monetary offering
Encountered a king of beggars at railway station, learned lesson about limitations in asking
Learned that Guru Shri Satyacharan Lahiri had prophesied his arrival as successor back in 1968
Studied under Shri Satyacharan Lahiri for five years, read Lahiri Mahasaya's diaries, meditated in his room
Became successor after Guru's departure
Received protection and guidance from unexpected fellow passenger, revealing father's legacy of justice
Promised dying father (1984) to repay aunt's gold loan of half a kilogram
Fulfilled promise 32 years later, returning gold to aunt and adding four gold bangles
Saved from train collision by guru's voice commanding him to leave tracks
Met the 'Saint of Boiled Potatoes' (old woman with Krishna darshan) who served him tea and potato
Encountered elderly beggar who offered him money instead of asking
1990: Visits Chitrakut, recalls Guru's story about a never-dying ascetic and inquires about him
During a visit to Varanasi, tells his guru about visions of future palaces, pinhole, tree, etc.
Guru predicts he will live in a palace-like place that is actually a cremation ground (smashan), and that if he maintains celibacy and practice, the Om symbol will form in his brain by age 44
First book read on yoga: father's Gheranda Samhita; devours Puranas
Father dies of cancer on 16 Sep 1984
One year later, traveling by train to Gwalior, meets an old man who offers him a seat – the encounter reinforces his spiritual path
Guru Satyacharan Lahiri leaves body on Jan 22, 1987, performing Omkar kriya
Sharma handles family duties at a Kali temple in Chuchura, Bengal
Sharma undergoes sacred thread ceremony (janeu) at temple festival
Sharma receives telegram of guru's death 20 days later
Sharma reaches Varanasi 12 days after cremation, performs final rituals
Sharma gives darshan in Asansol as new guru
Five bearded men examine Sharma's feet and find 'OM' sign on his soles
Sharma undergoes MRI at age 45; OM sign appears in brain, confirming guru's prediction
Sharma attends Maha Kumbh Mela in Allahabad in Feb 1988
Attended Kumbh Mela, bathed in cold river, sensed spiritual meaning of bath
Met a sadhu at Kumbh Mela who recognized him as the one who had initiated the sadhu's guru into Kriya
Stayed two days as VIP guest at Kumbh Mela camp
Traveled to Benares in March 1988 to help a student's job issue
On night of March 31 / April 1, 1988, in Benares ashram, met Babaji in person and received detailed Kriya instruction
Recorded notes of Babaji's teachings, including new techniques to be started after age 57
Returned home energized, but family skeptical; built a small cottage with loan from cousin
First spontaneous samadhi experience (described earlier, referenced here).
After nearly five years, second samadhi occurs, entering the realm of superconsciousness, with calm and peaceful experience unlike the first.
Experiences heightened senses, perceiving mental vibrations of animals and astral tastes; realizes bliss of samadhi as enjoyment of the world, understanding Shiva Sutras.
Samadhi becomes regular; learns to control entry and exit from the state; understands that lack of expectation is key to repetition.
Gains physical strength, golden aura, and lifts disciples overhead; internal energy requires daily practice with heavy clubs (mudgars).
Enters regular samadhi leading to realization of the Void consciousness and its omnipresence; becomes aware of time's nature and connection between mind and breath.
Encounters astral beings and learns to communicate on subtle planes; consciousness moves like tsunamis.
Performs longest planned samadhi (7.5 hours) on first week of December 1991; experiences prolonged recovery, concludes optimal duration is 1.5 hours.
Receives knowledge spontaneously during and outside samadhi, without need to focus on specific topics.
Experienced repeated samadhi and out-of-body experiences; learned to adapt to them.
Began writing commentaries on the Bhagavad Gita, describing near-death and post-mortem experiences.
Commentaries were published in August 1992; public readings held during Janmashtami.
Property disputes forced him to leave the farm; moved to Vrindavan with Devi and several disciples.
Arrived in Vrindavan on October 2 with cows and dogs; stayed at Goshala Nagar ashram hosted by Brahmachari Vairag Swarup.
Became a sensation in Vrindavan for his charisma, knowledge, and yogic sadhana.
Devotees injured his feet in fervent worship, leading him to avoid crowds
Learned to enter the Void (shunya) and return to the body
Practiced daily Bhagavad Gita reading as per parampara vow for years
Wrote a commentary on the Bhagavad Gita, experiencing dictation of knowledge
Realized that matter arises from the union of Time and Void
Had an encounter with Babaji that expanded his consciousness
Pondered the nature of Time and Void, recognizing them as conscious beings
Reflected on the Gita's teaching that Krishna is Time
Participated in Govardhan parikrama on 21 December (Dattatreya Purnima night) with about 30-35 disciples, walking nearly naked in extreme cold.
Experienced spontaneous empathy: others' physical pain manifested in his own body as a side effect of samadhi.
Helped by an elderly woman during the parikrama; later encountered a mysterious man who bowed at his bleeding feet.
Reflected on his name Shailendra meaning 'King of the Mountain', linked to Govardhan hill, and recalled how Shailendranath Mukherjee in Puri guided him to Kriya Yoga books and a teacher.
After the first parikrama, experienced a life change: wrote four books in a few weeks and found a 'palace'.
Settled in Govardhan in July 1993 after many unexpected events.
Observed a wealthy leprous girl completing dandavat parikrama, later healed.
Experienced the 'Inner Symphony': profound internal perception of body sounds and microcosmic universe during samadhi.
At age 33, after a quarrel with relatives, he left home and moved to an unfinished farmhouse, beginning a new life of solitude and practice.
On his first night at the farm, a worker built a makeshift stove from bricks and wild ingredients, teaching him that nature provides all needs; he tasted freedom.

During excavation, a massive termite mound was destroyed, revealing a black cobra; later that night, a three-meter-tall Naga appeared in a dream, asking him not to harm his children, and offering protection.
The next morning, a golden cobra with red eyes appeared on the veranda; he decided not to harm it and negotiated a peaceful coexistence.
One night in September, he saw two luminous, naked human figures (male and female) floating above the ground; they laughed and then disappeared, leaving him ecstatic.
Two blue-throated jays began visiting daily, perching on poles near his chair as if guarding him.
His mother sent his elder brother Gyanendra to check on him; Gyanendra found high-ranking officials serving at the farm and reported back that he was doing well.
A disciple brought two cows and a month-old calf named Gandhi; his cousin Devahuti visited and played with the calf.
During a farewell, two blue-throated jays landed on his head, which he interpreted as an auspicious sign.
He described his first samadhi to his Guru, who said it was just the beginning and advised him to continue practicing.
Living on the Govardhan hill smasana from September 1993, clearing the land of filth and dealing with local gangs through physical confrontations.
After a fight, ordered his wife to pack to leave, but their auto broke down near the temple within 100 meters of the smasana, and then a jeep also broke down.
Lal Baba's arrival at Sarnath ashram draws large crowds and media attention.
On Shivaratri, leads a march to Sarangnath Mahadev temple; police investigation resolved by gifting Bhagavad Gita.
Dehuti gives him a poem; he writes 19 poems in response, later compiling 52 into 'The Pilgrim'.
Compiles scattered notes into 'Thorns and Roses'.
Registers 'Siddha Siddhanta Yoga Academy'.
Visits Banamali Lahiri at Lahiri Mahasaya's old house; learns that Yogananda has reincarnated as a recluse near the Ganges.
Received a book of Alex Grey's art, which resonated with his own psychedelic experiences of cosmic consciousness
After intensive sadhana, realized that Matter arises from Void and that Void is conscious
Spontaneous parikrama of Govardhan where he understood the structure of the elements and material reality
Felt a great invisible presence follow him from Govardhan and settle nearby
During a séance with his cousin Devahuti in Mathura, a Siddha appeared and commanded him to begin writing commentaries on the Bhagavad Gita
Dictated commentaries on the Bhagavad Gita over 21 days, from January 14 to Vasanta Panchami eve
Led a march of yogis on Maha Shivaratri from Kālī temple to Gupteshwara Mahadeva shrine
Performed his first Abhishek at the Shiva temple, after which thousands spontaneously prostrated before him
Stays at an ashram in Vrindavan, observes political and religious corruption
Participates in a march for Ram temple in Ayodhya, refuses further political involvement
Witnesses a mysterious beggar who performs a grand bhandara and then disappears, suspecting a siddha
Intervenes to give alms to a beggar after a wealthy man refuses, citing karma
Receives disciples and, over four days, dictates commentary on Yoga Sutras of Patanjali (one chapter per day)
Completes commentary on Shiva Sutras in 17 hours, feeling the presence of Shiva, Shakti, and Dattatreya
Has a dream of a monkey-faced being (langur) who promises to take care of everything
Receives invitation from Lal Baba to stay at Shanti Peeth Ashram in Sarnath; accepts after deliberation
Meets Dalai Lama briefly in Sarnath, but is more focused on the kriva techniques from Babaji
Before leaving for Sarnath, performs a parikrama of Govardhan hill and asks Giriraj for a permanent home
Receives a garland from an astral female spirit at the Govardhan cremation ground (smashan), recognizing it as his home.
Purchases the royal smashan (Moksha Bhumi) in Govardhan from the Bharatpur royal family in June 1993.
Moves into the white house on the smashan, a building with 108 doorways, and experiences disturbances with a photo of Lahiri Mahasaya.
Recognizes the Govardhan hill and the white house from a childhood vision and a medium's vision in Mathura, confirming his destiny.

Moved to smashan (cremation ground) in Govardhan and began living there, despite multiple vehicle breakdowns trying to leave.
Postponed visiting a saint on inner instruction; the saint later fell into a coma.
Visited the dying saint Pandit ji at his wife's urging; the saint died that night.
Survived first harsh winter on the smashan with his wife.
Thwarted assassination plot by local gangsters because the boss's wife recognized his uncle as her husband's savior.
Fought and defeated a local gangster in a fistfight; that gangster was later killed by a bus.
Demonstrated superhuman strength by holding a 350kg stone beam to save worker Fateh, which convinced locals of his sainthood.
Began philanthropic work in Braj: providing water during drought, planting trees, installing water coolers at police station.
Lived at a cremation ground (smashan) with a five-thousand-year-old ritual pond
Experience of mysterious hissing sound from the pond and discovery of a giant serpent (Naga) guard
Encounter with the Naga on 27 January 1995, receiving blessings and protection
Rescued an unknown dying man found at the gates in summer 1997
Rescued a wounded bull (Bhola Shankar Tiwari) from Mathura
Observed monkeys bringing dead companions; performed last rites for a dying monkey in 2006
Performed a Puranic forgiveness ritual for a dying monkey holding straw in its mouth
Inherits father's land; sells it and receives share as inheritance.
Moves to Govardhan smashan (cremation ground) and begins renovation.
Runs out of funds to pay workers; trusts in divine plan; money arrives from a healed footballer.
Heals a former Mohun Bagan footballer via alchemical mercury pill.
Receives donation from footballer exactly equal to worker wages.
Declines commercial offers including a government paranormal research program.
June 1995: vultures gather and leave; portent of Maharaja Brijendra Singh's death.
Maharaja Brijendra Singh dies; cremated at the royal smashan now owned by Sharma.
Meets Prince Vishvendra Singh after cremation; they become friends.
Distributes copies of his Gita commentary to dispel rumors.
Years later, Jat elders visit and prostrate, saying 'The king has returned'.
Heard stories of people meeting Babaji, who described long spiritual discussions and advice on diet and career.
First personal meeting with Babaji at his home in winter, receiving a Sri Yantra and instructions for its installation.
Second meeting with Babaji revealed new perspectives and a sense of mission.
Occasional mental messages from Babaji every 4-5 years, containing visions and scenes with voiceover.
Last mental communication from Babaji in autumn 2019, showing Babaji living in Varanasi.
Received a buffalo horn as a gift for Shiva puja
Bull named Gandhiji arrived in 1991
Gandhiji became a companion for 14 years until death in 2005
Lived on a cremation ground (smashan) in Govardhan
Hosted ISKCON's centenary celebration of Srila Prabhupada in summer 1996
Received marriage proposals during the ISKCON festival, declined
Cared for over 300 abandoned cows in winter 2001
Built cow sheds and moved the herd
Collected 16 baroncha (hairballs) from happy cows
He did not refute rumors of royal lineage but kept knowledge of past lives to himself.
He helped catch a rare snake at one of the palaces, summoning the snake charmer Bhimnath.
He observed Bhimnath get bitten and apply traditional remedies; later researched theoretical implications of the event.
